name: webapp-testing
tagline: Guides writing native Playwright scripts to test local web apps: screenshots, DOM inspection, server lifecycle via with_server.py.
verdict: Solid, concrete testing skill: clear decision tree, worked examples, and deliberate context-window economy (scripts kept as black boxes). Weak spots: no scope exclusions (Q02) risking misfire against other browser-automation skills, Playwright dependency never declared or checked (H07), no version/changelog signal (H08), and only the early-inspection.

highlights.summary: No 'NOT for' / out-of-scope text anywhere in description or body, despite overlap risk with generic browser-automation or e2e-test skills.
Only the networkidle-before-inspection pitfall is addressed; server-start failure, missing Playwright, or selector-not-found are unaddressed.
Scripts silently assume Playwright (and its browser binaries) are installed; no requirements section, install step, or availability check.
